indicators.name: no flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: The absence of a flat surface at sitting height makes it impossible to set up a computer workstation.

indicators.name: upholstered lounge seating
indicators.rationale: The soft lounge seating without an adjacent surface forces an uncomfortable, non-ergonomic posture for laptop use.

indicators.name: high-traffic public lobby layout
indicators.rationale: The hotel lobby is a high-distraction, high-foot-traffic public space, making it incompatible with focused solo knowledge work.

indicators.name: ambient chandelier overhead lighting
indicators.rationale: Decorative ambient lighting provides no task illumination for screen work and may cause screen glare.

indicators.name: decorative plant arrangements
indicators.rationale: Decorative elements confirm this is a public reception space, not a work-focused environment.

reasoning_summary: This hotel lobby is fundamentally unsuitable for focused solo computer work. The lack of any desk surface, combined with the high-traffic public layout and ambient-only lighting, prevents productive workstation setup. A laptop could technically be balanced on one's lap on the sofa, but the environment is far from adequate for sustained work.
